search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG136 home blood pressure monitoring log technique review
site:dha.gov.ae hypertension telehealth home readings red flags follow-up
candidate_sources_rejected: home log used alone to diagnose hypertension or define control
automatic target, average interpretation, medicine change, or escalation
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Measurement validity and clinical context are required.
Targets, treatment, and escalation remain clinician decisions.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults using home blood-pressure monitoring; pregnancy, children, arrhythmia, severe readings, postural symptoms, frailty, CKD, and condition-specific targets require tailored pathways.
setting_applicability: Primary-care documentation and follow-up as qualified by the exact source and clinical context.
UAE_applicability: Direct DHA hypertension red-flag evidence supplemented by NICE measurement and HBPM guidance; UAE prescribing and follow-up pathways remain locally governed.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact HBPM section supports recording repeated home readings, measurement source, schedule, averages, and clinic comparison without automatically diagnosing hypertension.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting repeated clinic readings, ABPM or HBPM source and averages, result context, annual measurement in relevant diabetes care, and clinician review without automatically diagnosing hypertension.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact technique section supports clinician review of device validation, maintenance, environment, seated posture, supported arm, cuff size, pulse, and measured observations without inferring correct technique.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports validated and maintained devices, standardized measurement conditions, appropriate cuff size, pulse review, and clinician-recorded postural measurements.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA red-flag section supports clinician-assessed severe readings, confusion, visual change, chest pain, breathlessness, neurological symptoms, severe headache, and escalation concern without asserting absence.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists severe blood pressure, confusion, visual changes, chest pain, breathlessness, neurological symptoms, severe headache, and specialist referral criteria.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact monitoring section supports clinician-entered training discussion, target context, questions, plan, and arranged follow-up without generating a target or treatment change.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinic, home, or ambulatory blood-pressure monitoring, symptoms, medication and lifestyle discussion, and an annual review without generating targets, treatment, or advice.

unresolved_source_gaps: Device, technique, dates, readings, averages, symptoms, pregnancy, diagnosis, target, medicine, treatment response, escalation, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.

All previously supported GP legacy-item mappings were conservatively removed because exact item identity, direct support, and item-specific applicability were not independently established under the fail-closed contract.
